name: kr-book-01.pdf 검증 기준의 pdf-to-typst 변환 품질 고도화

userStories.title: Sample the first three pages to choose a default extraction strategy
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the converter to compare native extraction and OCR on only the first three pages so that it can choose a deterministic whole-document default without paying full-document dual OCR cost.
acceptanceCriteria: Use only pages 1-3 as the comparison sample when determining the document-wide default extraction strategy.
If the document has fewer than three pages, evaluate only the pages that exist.
Repeated runs on the same input choose the same default strategy.
Dual evaluation is limited to the sample pages instead of the whole document.
`cargo fmt --check` passes
`cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passes
`cargo test` passes
`cargo run -- ./data/kr-book-01.pdf /tmp/pdf-to-typst-kr-book-01-out --strict` passes
`typst compile /tmp/pdf-to-typst-kr-book-01-out/main.typ` passes
`--strict` execution uses an empty output directory
The ralph loop attempts whole-document render comparison and always rechecks the first three pages after each fix until quality is high

userStories.title: Handle incomplete sample OCR data without aborting conversion
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want missing OCR or partial extraction failures in the sample pages to degrade gracefully so that strategy selection does not stop the conversion.
acceptanceCriteria: If a sampled page has no OCR candidate or unsupported OCR input, the converter emits a warning and continues.
Partial extraction failures during sample evaluation fall back to the best remaining signal instead of aborting the document.
After sample evaluation completes, the chosen strategy is reused for the rest of the document.
No new user-facing CLI flags or arguments are introduced for sample evaluation behavior.
`cargo fmt --check` passes
`cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passes
`cargo test` passes
`cargo run -- ./data/kr-book-01.pdf /tmp/pdf-to-typst-kr-book-01-out --strict` passes
`typst compile /tmp/pdf-to-typst-kr-book-01-out/main.typ` passes
`--strict` execution uses an empty output directory
The ralph loop attempts whole-document render comparison and always rechecks the first three pages after each fix until quality is high

userStories.title: Preserve editable body text and reading order in Typst
userStories.description: As a reader, I want normal text content to remain editable Typst text so that the output stays searchable, reusable, and close to the source PDF.
acceptanceCriteria: On the first three pages of `kr-book-01.pdf`, body text appears in `main.typ` as Typst text literals rather than image assets.
The dominant reading order on the first three pages remains approximately top-to-bottom and left-to-right.
Extractable Korean text remains searchable and editable in the generated Typst output.
Ordinary body text, headings, and simple caption text are not replaced with raster assets.
`cargo fmt --check` passes
`cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passes
`cargo test` passes
`cargo run -- ./data/kr-book-01.pdf /tmp/pdf-to-typst-kr-book-01-out --strict` passes
`typst compile /tmp/pdf-to-typst-kr-book-01-out/main.typ` passes
`--strict` execution uses an empty output directory
The ralph loop attempts whole-document render comparison and always rechecks the first three pages after each fix until quality is high

userStories.title: Keep simple tables, figures, and captions on structured output paths
userStories.description: As a reader, I want simple tables, figures, and captions to stay on structured Typst output paths whenever possible so that only genuinely hard regions need image fallback.
acceptanceCriteria: Structurally simple tables continue through a structured Typst output path instead of automatic raster fallback.
Ordinary figure placement and simple captions remain represented without flattening unrelated text.
Simple figure and table captions stay associated with their content when detected.
Pages that contain non-text elements still keep unrelated text editable.
`cargo fmt --check` passes
`cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passes
`cargo test` passes
`cargo run -- ./data/kr-book-01.pdf /tmp/pdf-to-typst-kr-book-01-out --strict` passes
`typst compile /tmp/pdf-to-typst-kr-book-01-out/main.typ` passes
`--strict` execution uses an empty output directory
The ralph loop attempts whole-document render comparison and always rechecks the first three pages after each fix until quality is high

userStories.title: Rasterize only complex elements and ban whole-page fallback
userStories.description: As a reader, I want only structurally complex tables, diagrams, and caption clusters to fall back to element images so that difficult regions stay legible without flattening the entire page.
acceptanceCriteria: Element-level image fallback is used only when complexity signals such as failed structure extraction, merged cells, rotated structure, or nested caption clusters are detected.
When one element falls back to an image, unrelated text on the same page remains text output.
No page emits a whole-page raster fallback under any condition.
Any existing raster fallback behavior that conflicts with element-only fallback is removed or disabled.
`cargo fmt --check` passes
`cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passes
`cargo test` passes
`cargo run -- ./data/kr-book-01.pdf /tmp/pdf-to-typst-kr-book-01-out --strict` passes
`typst compile /tmp/pdf-to-typst-kr-book-01-out/main.typ` passes
`--strict` execution uses an empty output directory
The ralph loop attempts whole-document render comparison and always rechecks the first three pages after each fix until quality is high

userStories.title: Emit compileable and comparison-stable strict-mode output
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the generated Typst project for `kr-book-01.pdf` to compile cleanly in strict mode so that render comparison highlights meaningful regressions instead of broken output.
acceptanceCriteria: Running the strict sample conversion produces a project that compiles with `typst compile` without manual edits.
Paragraphs, tables, images, and captions keep roughly correct spatial relationships compared with the source PDF.
Minor kerning, tracking, or spacing differences are tolerated, but missing, duplicated, severely misplaced, or unreadable content is treated as failure.
The output structure stays stable enough to support repeatable comparison runs across the whole document.
`cargo fmt --check` passes
`cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passes
`cargo test` passes
`cargo run -- ./data/kr-book-01.pdf /tmp/pdf-to-typst-kr-book-01-out --strict` passes

The ralph loop attempts whole-document render comparison and always rechecks the first three pages after each fix until quality is high

userStories.title: Keep iterative validation in the ralph session without changing the CLI
userStories.description: As an operator, I want repeated validation to stay in the ralph session workflow so that converter quality can improve iteratively without adding retry orchestration to the CLI itself.
acceptanceCriteria: The `pdf-to-typst` binary does not add internal retry, self-healing, or session orchestration behavior.
Repeated validation uses the fixed sample `./data/kr-book-01.pdf` after each code change.
Validation attempts a whole-document render comparison while treating the first three pages as a mandatory gate.
The CLI surface and arguments remain unchanged while the external validation workflow is reflected in project documentation or task notes.

`cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passes
`cargo test` passes
